CP()                                                                      CP()

NNAAMMEE
       cp -

DDEESSCCRRIIPPTTIIOONN
       Copies a local file or S3 object to another location locally or in S3.

SSYYNNOOPPSSIISS

            cp
          <LocalPath> <S3Uri> or <S3Uri> <LocalPath> or <S3Uri> <S3Uri>
          [--dryrun]
          [--quiet]
          [--include <value>]
          [--exclude <value>]
          [--acl <value>]
          [--follow-symlinks | --no-follow-symlinks]
          [--no-guess-mime-type]
          [--sse <value>]
          [--sse-c <value>]
          [--sse-c-key <value>]
          [--sse-kms-key-id <value>]
          [--sse-c-copy-source <value>]
          [--sse-c-copy-source-key <value>]
          [--storage-class <value>]
          [--grants <value> [<value>...]]
          [--website-redirect <value>]
          [--content-type <value>]
          [--cache-control <value>]
          [--content-disposition <value>]
          [--content-encoding <value>]
          [--content-language <value>]
          [--expires <value>]
          [--source-region <value>]
          [--only-show-errors]
          [--no-progress]
          [--progress-frequency <value>]
          [--progress-multiline]
          [--page-size <value>]
          [--ignore-glacier-warnings]
          [--force-glacier-transfer]
          [--request-payer <value>]
          [--checksum-mode <value>]
          [--checksum-algorithm <value>]
          [--metadata <value>]
          [--copy-props <value>]
          [--metadata-directive <value>]
          [--expected-size <value>]
          [--recursive]
          [--case-conflict <value>]
          [--no-overwrite]
          [--debug]
          [--endpoint-url <value>]
          [--no-verify-ssl]
          [--no-paginate]
          [--output <value>]
          [--query <value>]
          [--profile <value>]
          [--region <value>]
          [--version <value>]
          [--color <value>]
          [--no-sign-request]
          [--ca-bundle <value>]
          [--cli-read-timeout <value>]
          [--cli-connect-timeout <value>]
          [--cli-binary-format <value>]
          [--no-cli-pager]
          [--cli-auto-prompt]
          [--no-cli-auto-prompt]
          [--cli-error-format <value>]

OOPPTTIIOONNSS
       ppaatthhss (string)

       ----ddrryyrruunn (boolean) Displays the operations that would be performed
       using the specified command without actually running them.

       ----qquuiieett (boolean) Does not display the operations performed from the
       specified command.

       ----iinncclluuddee (string) Don't exclude files or objects in the command that
       match the specified pattern. See _U_s_e _o_f _E_x_c_l_u_d_e _a_n_d _I_n_c_l_u_d_e _F_i_l_t_e_r_s for
       details.

       ----eexxcclluuddee (string) Exclude all files or objects from the command that
       matches the specified pattern.

       ----aaccll (string) Sets the ACL for the object when the command is
       performed. If you use this parameter you must have the
       "s3:PutObjectAcl" permission included in the list of actions for your
       IAM policy. Only accepts values of pprriivvaattee, ppuubblliicc--rreeaadd,
       ppuubblliicc--rreeaadd--wwrriittee, aauutthheennttiiccaatteedd--rreeaadd, aawwss--eexxeecc--rreeaadd,
       bbuucckkeett--oowwnneerr--rreeaadd, bbuucckkeett--oowwnneerr--ffuullll--ccoonnttrrooll and lloogg--ddeelliivveerryy--wwrriittee.
       See _C_a_n_n_e_d _A_C_L for details

       ----ffoollllooww--ssyymmlliinnkkss | ----nnoo--ffoollllooww--ssyymmlliinnkkss (boolean) Symbolic links are
       followed only when uploading to S3 from the local filesystem. Note that
       S3 does not support symbolic links, so the contents of the link target
       are uploaded under the name of the link. When neither ----ffoollllooww--ssyymmlliinnkkss
       nor ----nnoo--ffoollllooww--ssyymmlliinnkkss is specified, the default is to follow
       symlinks.

       ----nnoo--gguueessss--mmiimmee--ttyyppee (boolean) Do not try to guess the mime type for
       uploaded files. By default the mime type of a file is guessed when it
       is uploaded.

       ----ssssee (string) Specifies server-side encryption of the object in S3.
       Valid values are AAEESS225566 and aawwss::kkmmss. If the parameter is specified but
       no value is provided, AAEESS225566 is used.

       ----ssssee--cc (string) Specifies server-side encryption using customer
       provided keys of the the object in S3. AAEESS225566 is the only valid value.
       If the parameter is specified but no value is provided, AAEESS225566 is used.
       If you provide this value, ----ssssee--cc--kkeeyy must be specified as well.

       ----ssssee--cc--kkeeyy (blob) The customer-provided encryption key to use to
       server-side encrypt the object in S3. If you provide this value,
       ----ssssee--cc must be specified as well. The key provided should nnoott be
       base64 encoded.

       ----ssssee--kkmmss--kkeeyy--iidd (string) The customer-managed AWS Key Management
       Service (KMS) key ID that should be used to server-side encrypt the
       object in S3. You should only provide this parameter if you are using a
       customer managed customer master key (CMK) and not the AWS managed KMS
       CMK.

       ----ssssee--cc--ccooppyy--ssoouurrccee (string) This parameter should only be specified
       when copying an S3 object that was encrypted server-side with a
       customer-provided key. It specifies the algorithm to use when
       decrypting the source object. AAEESS225566 is the only valid value. If the
       parameter is specified but no value is provided, AAEESS225566 is used. If you
       provide this value, ----ssssee--cc--ccooppyy--ssoouurrccee--kkeeyy must be specified as well.

       ----ssssee--cc--ccooppyy--ssoouurrccee--kkeeyy (blob) This parameter should only be specified
       when copying an S3 object that was encrypted server-side with a
       customer-provided key. Specifies the customer-provided encryption key
       for Amazon S3 to use to decrypt the source object. The encryption key
       provided must be one that was used when the source object was created.
       If you provide this value, ----ssssee--cc--ccooppyy--ssoouurrccee be specified as well.
       The key provided should nnoott be base64 encoded.

       ----ssttoorraaggee--ccllaassss (string) The type of storage to use for the object.
       Valid choices are: STANDARD | REDUCED_REDUNDANCY | STANDARD_IA |
       ONEZONE_IA | INTELLIGENT_TIERING | GLACIER | DEEP_ARCHIVE | GLACIER_IR.
       Defaults to 'STANDARD'

       ----ggrraannttss (string)
          Grant specific permissions to individual users or groups. You can
          supply a list of grants of the form

             --grants Permission=Grantee_Type=Grantee_ID [Permission=Grantee_Type=Grantee_ID ...]

          To specify the same permission type for multiple grantees, specify
          the permission as such as

             --grants Permission=Grantee_Type=Grantee_ID,Grantee_Type=Grantee_ID,...

          Each value contains the following elements:

          +o PPeerrmmiissssiioonn - Specifies the granted permissions, and can be set to
            read, readacl, writeacl, or full.

          +o GGrraanntteeee__TTyyppee - Specifies how the grantee is to be identified, and
            can be set to uri or id.

          +o GGrraanntteeee__IIDD - Specifies the grantee based on Grantee_Type. The
            GGrraanntteeee__IIDD value can be one of:

            +o uurrii - The group's URI. For more information, see _W_h_o _I_s _a
              _G_r_a_n_t_e_e_?

            +o iidd - The account's canonical ID

          For more information on Amazon S3 access control, see _A_c_c_e_s_s _C_o_n_t_r_o_l

       ----wweebbssiittee--rreeddiirreecctt (string) If the bucket is configured as a website,
       redirects requests for this object to another object in the same bucket
       or to an external URL. Amazon S3 stores the value of this header in the
       object metadata.

       ----ccoonntteenntt--ttyyppee (string) Specify an explicit content type for this
       operation. This value overrides any guessed mime types.

       ----ccaacchhee--ccoonnttrrooll (string) Specifies caching behavior along the
       request/reply chain.

       ----ccoonntteenntt--ddiissppoossiittiioonn (string) Specifies presentational information for
       the object.

       ----ccoonntteenntt--eennccooddiinngg (string) Specifies what content encodings have been
       applied to the object and thus what decoding mechanisms must be applied
       to obtain the media-type referenced by the Content-Type header field.

       ----ccoonntteenntt--llaanngguuaaggee (string) The language the content is in.

       ----eexxppiirreess (string) The date and time at which the object is no longer
       cacheable.

       ----ssoouurrccee--rreeggiioonn (string) When transferring objects from an s3 bucket to
       an s3 bucket, this specifies the region of the source bucket. Note the
       region specified by ----rreeggiioonn or through configuration of the CLI refers
       to the region of the destination bucket. If ----ssoouurrccee--rreeggiioonn is not
       specified the region of the source will be the same as the region of
       the destination bucket.

       ----oonnllyy--sshhooww--eerrrroorrss (boolean) Only errors and warnings are displayed.
       All other output is suppressed.

       ----nnoo--pprrooggrreessss (boolean) File transfer progress is not displayed. This
       flag is only applied when the quiet and only-show-errors flags are not
       provided.

       ----pprrooggrreessss--ffrreeqquueennccyy (integer) Number of seconds to wait before
       updating file transfer progress. This flag is only applied when the
       quiet and only-show-errors flags are not provided.

       ----pprrooggrreessss--mmuullttiilliinnee (boolean) Show progress on multiple lines.

       ----ppaaggee--ssiizzee (integer) The number of results to return in each response
       to a list operation. The default value is 1000 (the maximum allowed).
       Using a lower value may help if an operation times out.

       ----iiggnnoorree--ggllaacciieerr--wwaarrnniinnggss (boolean) Turns off glacier warnings.
       Warnings about an operation that cannot be performed because it
       involves copying, downloading, or moving a glacier object will no
       longer be printed to standard error and will no longer cause the return
       code of the command to be 22.

       ----ffoorrccee--ggllaacciieerr--ttrraannssffeerr (boolean) Forces a transfer request on all
       Glacier objects in a sync or recursive copy.

       ----rreeqquueesstt--ppaayyeerr (string) Confirms that the requester knows that they
       will be charged for the request. Bucket owners need not specify this
       parameter in their requests. Documentation on downloading objects from
       requester pays buckets can be found at
       _h_t_t_p_:_/_/_d_o_c_s_._a_w_s_._a_m_a_z_o_n_._c_o_m_/_A_m_a_z_o_n_S_3_/_l_a_t_e_s_t_/_d_e_v_/_O_b_j_e_c_t_s_i_n_R_e_q_u_e_s_t_e_r_P_a_y_s_B_u_c_k_e_t_s_._h_t_m_l

       ----cchheecckkssuumm--mmooddee (string) To retrieve the checksum, this mode must be
       enabled. If the object has a checksum, it will be verified.

       ----cchheecckkssuumm--aallggoorriitthhmm (string) Indicates the algorithm used to create
       the checksum for the object.

       ----mmeettaaddaattaa (map) A map of metadata to store with the objects in S3.
       This will be applied to every object which is part of this request. In
       a sync, this means that files which haven't changed won't receive the
       new metadata. key -> (string)
          value -> (string)

       Shorthand Syntax:

          KeyName1=string,KeyName2=string

       JSON Syntax:

          {"string": "string"
            ...}

       ----ccooppyy--pprrooppss (string) Determines which properties are copied from the
       source S3 object. This parameter only applies for S3 to S3 copies.
       Valid values are:

          +o nnoonnee - Do not copy any of the properties from the source S3
            object.

          +o mmeettaaddaattaa--ddiirreeccttiivvee - Copies the following properties from the
            source S3 object: ccoonntteenntt--ttyyppee, ccoonntteenntt--llaanngguuaaggee,
            ccoonntteenntt--eennccooddiinngg, ccoonntteenntt--ddiissppoossiittiioonn, ccaacchhee--ccoonnttrrooll, ----eexxppiirreess,
            and mmeettaaddaattaa

          +o ddeeffaauulltt - The default value. Copies tags and properties covered
            under the mmeettaaddaattaa--ddiirreeccttiivvee value from the source S3 object.

          In order to copy the appropriate properties for multipart copies,
          some of the options may require additional API calls if a multipart
          copy is involved. Specifically:

          +o mmeettaaddaattaa--ddiirreeccttiivvee may require additional HHeeaaddOObbjjeecctt API calls.

          +o ddeeffaauulltt may require additional HHeeaaddOObbjjeecctt, GGeettOObbjjeeccttTTaaggggiinngg, and
            PPuuttOObbjjeeccttTTaaggggiinngg API calls. Note this list of API calls may grow
            in the future in order to ensure multipart copies preserve the
            exact properties a CCooppyyOObbjjeecctt API call would preserve.

          If you want to guarantee no additional API calls are made other than
          than the ones needed to perform the actual copy, set this option to
          nnoonnee.

       ----mmeettaaddaattaa--ddiirreeccttiivvee (string) Sets the xx--aammzz--mmeettaaddaattaa--ddiirreeccttiivvee header
       for CopyObject operations. It is recommended to use the ----ccooppyy--pprrooppss
       parameter instead to control copying of metadata properties. If
       ----mmeettaaddaattaa--ddiirreeccttiivvee is set, the ----ccooppyy--pprrooppss parameter will be
       disabled and will have no affect on the transfer.

       ----eexxppeecctteedd--ssiizzee (string) This argument specifies the expected size of a
       stream in terms of bytes. Note that this argument is needed only when a
       stream is being uploaded to s3 and the size is larger than 50GB.
       Failure to include this argument under these conditions may result in a
       failed upload due to too many parts in upload.

       ----rreeccuurrssiivvee (boolean) Command is performed on all files or objects
       under the specified directory or prefix.

       ----ccaassee--ccoonnfflliicctt (string) Configures behavior when attempting to
       download multiple objects whose keys differ only by case, which can
       cause undefined behavior on case-insensitive filesystems. This
       parameter only applies for commands that perform multiple S3 to local
       downloads. See _H_a_n_d_l_i_n_g _c_a_s_e _c_o_n_f_l_i_c_t_s for details. Valid values are:

          +o eerrrroorr - Raise an error and abort downloads.

          +o wwaarrnn - Emit a warning and download the object.

          +o sskkiipp - Skip downloading the object.

          +o iiggnnoorree - The default value. Ignore the conflict and download the
            object.

       ----nnoo--oovveerrwwrriittee (boolean) This flag prevents overwriting of files at the
       destination. With this flag, only files not present at the destination
       will be transferred.

GGLLOOBBAALL OOPPTTIIOONNSS
       ----ddeebbuugg (boolean)

       Turn on debug logging.

       ----eennddppooiinntt--uurrll (string)

       Override command's default URL with the given URL.

       ----nnoo--vveerriiffyy--ssssll (boolean)

       By default, the AWS CLI uses SSL when communicating with AWS services.
       For each SSL connection, the AWS CLI will verify SSL certificates. This
       option overrides the default behavior of verifying SSL certificates.

       ----nnoo--ppaaggiinnaattee (boolean)

       Disable automatic pagination. If automatic pagination is disabled, the
       AWS CLI will only make one call, for the first page of results.

       ----oouuttppuutt (string)

       The formatting style for command output.

       +o json

       +o text

       +o table

       +o yaml

       +o yaml-stream

       +o off

       ----qquueerryy (string)

       A JMESPath query to use in filtering the response data.

       ----pprrooffiillee (string)

       Use a specific profile from your credential file.

       ----rreeggiioonn (string)

       The region to use. Overrides config/env settings.

       ----vveerrssiioonn (string)

       Display the version of this tool.

       ----ccoolloorr (string)

       Turn on/off color output.

       +o on

       +o off

       +o auto

       ----nnoo--ssiiggnn--rreeqquueesstt (boolean)

       Do not sign requests. Credentials will not be loaded if this argument
       is provided.

       ----ccaa--bbuunnddllee (string)

       The CA certificate bundle to use when verifying SSL certificates.
       Overrides config/env settings.

       ----ccllii--rreeaadd--ttiimmeeoouutt (int)

       The maximum socket read time in seconds. If the value is set to 0, the
       socket read will be blocking and not timeout. The default value is 60
       seconds.

       ----ccllii--ccoonnnneecctt--ttiimmeeoouutt (int)

       The maximum socket connect time in seconds. If the value is set to 0,
       the socket connect will be blocking and not timeout. The default value
       is 60 seconds.

       ----ccllii--bbiinnaarryy--ffoorrmmaatt (string)

       The formatting style to be used for binary blobs. The default format is
       base64. The base64 format expects binary blobs to be provided as a
       base64 encoded string. The raw-in-base64-out format preserves
       compatibility with AWS CLI V1 behavior and binary values must be passed
       literally. When providing contents from a file that map to a binary
       blob ffiilleebb:://// will always be treated as binary and use the file
       contents directly regardless of the ccllii--bbiinnaarryy--ffoorrmmaatt setting. When
       using ffiillee:://// the file contents will need to properly formatted for the
       configured ccllii--bbiinnaarryy--ffoorrmmaatt.

       +o base64

       +o raw-in-base64-out

       ----nnoo--ccllii--ppaaggeerr (boolean)

       Disable cli pager for output.

       ----ccllii--aauuttoo--pprroommpptt (boolean)

       Automatically prompt for CLI input parameters.

       ----nnoo--ccllii--aauuttoo--pprroommpptt (boolean)

       Disable automatically prompt for CLI input parameters.

       ----ccllii--eerrrroorr--ffoorrmmaatt (string)

       The formatting style for error output. By default, errors are displayed
       in enhanced format.

       +o legacy

       +o json

       +o yaml

       +o text

       +o table

       +o enhanced

EEXXAAMMPPLLEESS
       NNOOTTEE::
          To use the following examples, you must have the AWS CLI installed
          and configured. See the _G_e_t_t_i_n_g _s_t_a_r_t_e_d _g_u_i_d_e in the _A_W_S _C_L_I _U_s_e_r
          _G_u_i_d_e for more information.

          Unless otherwise stated, all examples have unix-like quotation
          rules. These examples will need to be adapted to your terminal's
          quoting rules. See _U_s_i_n_g _q_u_o_t_a_t_i_o_n _m_a_r_k_s _w_i_t_h _s_t_r_i_n_g_s in the _A_W_S _C_L_I
          _U_s_e_r _G_u_i_d_e .

       EExxaammppllee 11:: CCooppyyiinngg aa llooccaall ffiillee ttoo SS33

       The following ccpp command copies a single file to a specified bucket and
       key:

          aws s3 cp test.txt s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/test2.txt

       Output:

          upload: test.txt to s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/test2.txt

       EExxaammppllee 22:: CCooppyyiinngg aa llooccaall ffiillee ttoo SS33 wwiitthh aann eexxppiirraattiioonn ddaattee

       The following ccpp command copies a single file to a specified bucket and
       key that expires at the specified ISO 8601 timestamp:

          aws s3 cp test.txt s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/test2.txt \
              --expires 2014-10-01T20:30:00Z

       Output:

          upload: test.txt to s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/test2.txt

       EExxaammppllee 33:: CCooppyyiinngg aa ffiillee ffrroomm SS33 ttoo SS33

       The following ccpp command copies a single s3 object to a specified
       bucket and key:

          aws s3 cp s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/test.txt s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/test2.txt

       Output:

          copy: s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/test.txt to s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/test2.txt

       EExxaammppllee 44:: CCooppyyiinngg aann SS33 oobbjjeecctt ttoo aa llooccaall ffiillee

       The following ccpp command copies a single object to a specified file
       locally:

          aws s3 cp s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/test.txt test2.txt

       Output:

          download: s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/test.txt to test2.txt

       EExxaammppllee 55:: CCooppyyiinngg aann SS33 oobbjjeecctt ffrroomm oonnee bbuucckkeett ttoo aannootthheerr

       The following ccpp command copies a single object to a specified bucket
       while retaining its original name:

          aws s3 cp s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/test.txt s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket2/

       Output:

          copy: s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/test.txt to s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket2/test.txt

       EExxaammppllee 66:: RReeccuurrssiivveellyy ccooppyyiinngg SS33 oobbjjeeccttss ttoo aa llooccaall ddiirreeccttoorryy

       When passed with the parameter ----rreeccuurrssiivvee, the following ccpp command
       recursively copies all objects under a specified prefix and bucket to a
       specified directory.  In this example, the bucket aammzznn--ss33--ddeemmoo--bbuucckkeett
       has the objects tteesstt11..ttxxtt and tteesstt22..ttxxtt:

          aws s3 cp s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket . \
              --recursive

       Output:

          download: s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/test1.txt to test1.txt
          download: s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/test2.txt to test2.txt

       EExxaammppllee 77:: RReeccuurrssiivveellyy ccooppyyiinngg llooccaall ffiilleess ttoo SS33

       When passed with the parameter ----rreeccuurrssiivvee, the following ccpp command
       recursively copies all files under a specified directory to a specified
       bucket and prefix while excluding some files by using an ----eexxcclluuddee
       parameter.  In this example, the directory mmyyDDiirr has the files
       tteesstt11..ttxxtt and tteesstt22..jjppgg:

          aws s3 cp myDir s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/ \
              --recursive \
              --exclude "*.jpg"

       Output:

          upload: myDir/test1.txt to s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/test1.txt

       EExxaammppllee 88:: RReeccuurrssiivveellyy ccooppyyiinngg SS33 oobbjjeeccttss ttoo aannootthheerr bbuucckkeett

       When passed with the parameter ----rreeccuurrssiivvee, the following ccpp command
       recursively copies all objects under a specified bucket to another
       bucket while excluding some objects by using an ----eexxcclluuddee parameter.
       In this example, the bucket aammzznn--ss33--ddeemmoo--bbuucckkeett has the objects
       tteesstt11..ttxxtt and aannootthheerr//tteesstt11..ttxxtt:

          aws s3 cp s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/ s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket2/ \
              --recursive \
              --exclude "another/*"

       Output:

          copy: s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/test1.txt to s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket2/test1.txt

       You can combine ----eexxcclluuddee and ----iinncclluuddee options to copy only objects
       that match a pattern, excluding all others:

          aws s3 cp s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/logs/ s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket2/logs/ \
              --recursive \
              --exclude "*" \
              --include "*.log"

       Output:

          copy: s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/logs/test/test.log to s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket2/logs/test/test.log
          copy: s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/logs/test3.log to s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket2/logs/test3.log

       EExxaammppllee 99:: SSeettttiinngg tthhee AAcccceessss CCoonnttrrooll LLiisstt ((AACCLL)) wwhhiillee ccooppyyiinngg aann SS33
       oobbjjeecctt

       The following ccpp command copies a single object to a specified bucket
       and key while setting the ACL to ppuubblliicc--rreeaadd--wwrriittee:

          aws s3 cp s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/test.txt s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/test2.txt \
              --acl public-read-write

       Output:

          copy: s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/test.txt to s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/test2.txt

       Note that if you're using the ----aaccll option, ensure that any associated
       IAM policies include the ""ss33::PPuuttOObbjjeeccttAAccll"" action:

          aws iam get-user-policy \
              --user-name myuser \
              --policy-name mypolicy

       Output:

          {
              "UserName": "myuser",
              "PolicyName": "mypolicy",
              "PolicyDocument": {
                  "Version": "2012-10-17",
                  "Statement": [
                      {
                          "Action": [
                              "s3:PutObject",
                              "s3:PutObjectAcl"
                          ],
                          "Resource": [
                              "arn:aws:s3:::amzn-s3-demo-bucket/*"
                          ],
                          "Effect": "Allow",
                          "Sid": "Stmt1234567891234"
                      }
                  ]
              }
          }

       EExxaammppllee 1100:: GGrraannttiinngg ppeerrmmiissssiioonnss ffoorr aann SS33 oobbjjeecctt

       The following ccpp command illustrates the use of the ----ggrraannttss option to
       grant read access to all users identified by URI and full control to a
       specific user identified by their Canonical ID:

          aws s3 cp file.txt s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/ --grants read=uri=http://acs.amazonaws.com/groups/global/AllUsers full=id=79a59df900b949e55d96a1e698fbacedfd6e09d98eacf8f8d5218e7cd47ef2be

       Output:

          upload: file.txt to s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/file.txt

       EExxaammppllee 1111:: UUppllooaaddiinngg aa llooccaall ffiillee ssttrreeaamm ttoo SS33

       WWAARRNNIINNGG::
          PowerShell may alter the encoding of or add a CRLF to piped input.

       The following ccpp command uploads a local file stream from standard
       input to a specified bucket and key:

          aws s3 cp - s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/stream.txt

       EExxaammppllee 1122:: UUppllooaaddiinngg aa llooccaall ffiillee ssttrreeaamm tthhaatt iiss llaarrggeerr tthhaann 5500GGBB ttoo
       SS33

       The following ccpp command uploads a 51GB local file stream from standard
       input to a specified bucket and key.  The ----eexxppeecctteedd--ssiizzee option must
       be provided, or the upload may fail when it reaches the default part
       limit of 10,000:

          aws s3 cp - s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/stream.txt --expected-size 54760833024

       EExxaammppllee 1133:: DDoowwnnllooaaddiinngg aann SS33 oobbjjeecctt aass aa llooccaall ffiillee ssttrreeaamm

       WWAARRNNIINNGG::
          PowerShell may alter the encoding of or add a CRLF to piped or
          redirected output.

       The following ccpp command downloads an S3 object locally as a stream to
       standard output. Downloading as a stream is not currently compatible
       with the ----rreeccuurrssiivvee parameter:

          aws s3 cp s3://amzn-s3-demo-bucket/stream.txt -

       EExxaammppllee 1144:: UUppllooaaddiinngg ttoo aann SS33 aacccceessss ppooiinntt

       The following ccpp command uploads a single file (mmyyddoocc..ttxxtt) to the
       access point (mmyyaacccceessssppooiinntt) at the key (mmyykkeeyy):

          aws s3 cp mydoc.txt s3://arn:aws:s3:us-west-2:123456789012:accesspoint/myaccesspoint/mykey

       Output:

          upload: mydoc.txt to s3://arn:aws:s3:us-west-2:123456789012:accesspoint/myaccesspoint/mykey

       EExxaammppllee 1155:: DDoowwnnllooaaddiinngg ffrroomm aann SS33 aacccceessss ppooiinntt

       The following ccpp command downloads a single object (mmyykkeeyy) from the
       access point (mmyyaacccceessssppooiinntt) to the local file (mmyyddoocc..ttxxtt):

          aws s3 cp s3://arn:aws:s3:us-west-2:123456789012:accesspoint/myaccesspoint/mykey mydoc.txt

       Output:

          download: s3://arn:aws:s3:us-west-2:123456789012:accesspoint/myaccesspoint/mykey to mydoc.txt

                                                                          CP()
